description: Fix five genuine bugs and code quality issues in the MCP server: a subprocess that can hang forever, an unhandled promise rejection, an unbounded array, unvalidated input, and redundant I/O.

userStories.title: Add SIGKILL fallback and timeout guard to runNpmGlobal subprocess handling
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want npm subprocess operations to never hang indefinitely so that the MCP server remains responsive even when an npm process becomes unresponsive.
acceptanceCriteria: After proc.kill() fires (60s timeout), a secondary 5-second timer sends SIGKILL if the process has not exited
The entire runNpmGlobal function rejects with a timeout error rather than hanging indefinitely if the subprocess does not exit within the secondary timeout
All timers are properly cleaned up on normal (non-timeout) exit paths
The error thrown on timeout includes a clear message indicating the npm command timed out

userStories.title: Add .catch() to void sendSyncFull in WebSocket open handler
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want all fire-and-forget promises to have error handlers so that unhandled promise rejections do not crash the server process.
acceptanceCriteria: The `void sendSyncFull(state).then(...)` call in the WebSocket open handler (http-routes.ts) has a `.catch()` that logs the error via the logger
The catch handler does not re-throw (fire-and-forget pattern)
The pattern matches the existing pattern in reload.ts line 152 which already uses `.catch()` correctly

userStories.title: Validate ConfirmationScope before casting in handleConfirmationResponse
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Allow Always' confirmation action to work reliably so that clicking it actually persists the permission for the session instead of silently failing when the extension sends an unexpected scope value.
acceptanceCriteria: The scope value received from the extension is validated against the set of valid ConfirmationScope values ('tool_domain', 'tool_all', 'domain_all') before being used
Invalid scope values fall back to the default 'tool_domain' (same as current default)
A log.warn is emitted when an invalid scope value is received, to aid debugging
The type assertion `as ConfirmationScope` is no longer applied to unvalidated string input

userStories.title: Deduplicate sessionPermissions array to prevent unbounded growth
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want session permission rules to be deduplicated so that the sessionPermissions array does not grow without bound during long-running sessions with many 'Allow Always' actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Before pushing a new SessionPermissionRule, the code checks if an equivalent rule already exists (same tool, domain, and scope)
Duplicate rules are not added to the array
The deduplication check uses the correct semantics: null values in tool/domain fields are handled correctly (e.g., a rule with tool=null matches another rule with tool=null)
The isSessionAllowed function behavior is unchanged

userStories.title: Remove redundant loadSecret() call in index.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the startup code to not perform redundant I/O so that the initialization path is clean and each operation happens in exactly one place.
acceptanceCriteria: The `state.wsSecret = await loadSecret()` call on line 148 of index.ts is removed
The `loadSecret` import in index.ts is removed (no longer used)
The `loadSecret()` call in reloadCore (reload.ts line 201) remains as the single source of truth for loading the WebSocket secret
The comment on lines 146-147 of index.ts is removed since it is no longer relevant
knip does not report loadSecret as unused in index.ts
